Off Calendar Nov-13-2023 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-03-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe appeared by zoom for Jo Yvonne Timmons (petitioner).
Chinita Hankins appeared in propria persona.

The court noted that most of the estate was distributed in 2019, that a small reserve was left, that this petition needed to be filed because of the passing of the administrator and asked why the deadline to file objections had not been met. Mrs. Hankins stated that she sent her objections by certified mail. Mrs. Hankins also presented oral argument to support her position with regard to her father being a biological son of the decedent, incorrect names having been used in the petition, fraudulent statements being in the petition, her inheritance as well as other family members inheritance having been stolen, there having been undue influence, the codicil being fraudulent, etc. The court noted that the document of final distribution was filed on March 7, 2019 more than four years ago and that the time to attack the proceedings is now over. The court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed (order signed). Status of the reserve is set for November 13, 2023.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Choi. Not reported.

The petition for letters of administration with will annexed is continued to July 03, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The objector to file a written objection by June 20, 2023 and the petitioner to file a response by June 26, 2023.

Judge: Ross Moody: Clerk: Ateesh Datt: Deputy: Singh: Not reported.

Off Calendar Feb-14-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: In Propria Persona Treana Wilson is present. Daniel Burn for Thomas Wayne (personal representative) is present.

Mr. Burn informed the court that he has called Treana prior to reading the tentative ruling, that he does not contest to the tentative ruling and submitted two forms of proposed orders. Treana stated that she no longer has an attorney and is considering dismissing her claim. The Court advised Treana to take her time to think about her options and to not feel pressured.

* The Court takes a 10 minutes recess.

* The matter is recalled.

Treana Wilson verbally dismissed her will petition claim. Mr. Burn waived sanctions. The Court continued the accounting status hearing set for February 21, 2018 to March 07, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.


Judge: John K. Stewart, Clerk: N. Wong, Deputy: V. Jung. Not Reported.

Appearances: Vincent Yu for Treana Wilson (Objecting Party).
Daniel Byrne for and with Thomas Piggee (Respondent).

The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved as Counsel (subject to review of the order and subject to Mr. Yu providing information to Mr. Byrne). The Petition to Determine Validity of the Will, etc. and the status report are continued to February 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Oct-04-2017 Continued To Nov-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Thomas Piggee (Executor).
James O'Neil for and with Treana Wilson (Beneficiary).

Mr. O'Neil stated that he has submitted a petition to determine validity of the will, that he discussed mediation, and that Ms. O'Keeffe is not willing to participate in mediation and wants to proceed. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that she is meeting with litigation counsel and that if appropriate, she will do a motion for summary judgment. The status report, etc. is continued to November 20, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Continued To Oct-04-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary O'Keeffe for and with Thomas Piggee.
James O'Niel for and with Treana Wilson (Beneficiary).

Mr. O'Niel stated that he received the analysis of the signature by the Expert yesterday, that the Expert authenticated the signature, and that he was also told by the Expert that the first two pages were likely created after the second two pages of the four page will. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that this is new information that she is just receiving, agrees with the Court that the matter should be set for trial and stated that she has to file a petition for authority to hire litigation counsel. The status report is continued to October 4, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the will contest, and to see if any discovery needs to be completed, if the parties are able to resolve the matter, and if Ms. Wilson is going to oppose the motion to withdraw or have new Counsel.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jul-24-2017 Continued To Sep-06-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Mary O'Keeffe for and with Thomas Piggee (Executor).
Vincent Yu for and with Treana Wilson (Objector).

Mr. Yu stated that he intends on filing an official complaint within two weeks, that he is interested in mediation, and that his Client believes that the will that was presented was fraudulently signed. Mr. Yu also stated that they are looking into getting a hand writing expert to review the will, and that if his Client is satisfied with the analysis of the will, she will withdraw her objection. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that discovery was served on Mr. O'Neil, that the request for admissions was not responded to and that time for responses is over. Mr. Yu stated that he was not aware of any discovery. Ms. Wilson stated that she presented her theory about the will to her Attorney in 2015, that the Attorney did nothing about it, that she received a letter from Ms. O'Keeffe in May and that she had not heard from her Attorney in one year. The status report, etc. is continued to September 6, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery and status on the analysis of the signature on the will.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with Thomas Piggee. James O'Neil & Vincent Yu for and with Treana Wilson.

Mr. O'Neil reported that the summons has been served on everyone except Chilita Galvan, that they have attempted 6 times, that they have been in phone contact with Chilita and confirmed her address, that she has promised to let them serve her but then is evasive. Ms. O'Keeffe addressed the amended will contest, that contestants believe there is a subsequent will or codicil, that Mr. Hankins died in August, that the house is vacant and that there are bills to pay, suggest appointing Mr. Piggee on the will that they have, give time to the contestant to find the other will and that no assets will be distributed and that nothing will be gotten rid of. Mr. O'Neil stated that there is a lack of trust, that there is no faith in the documents that have been produced and that they would like to be sent to mediation to reach a solution and move forward. The Court asked if a stipulation could emerge to solve the immediate issues. Mr. O'Neil stated that they would need clarity on the powers being sought and that they should be limited powers. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that there currently is no subsequent document, that there is nothing to mediate, that mediation will not produce a subsequent document, requested that Mr. Piggee be appointed with a clear order that nothing can be sold and then they can talk. The Court suggested a stipulated order that sets forth limited powers and then get everyone served. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that they can mediate if there is something to mediate. The Court stated that there would be language in the order that would circumscribe exactly what Mr. Piggee could do. Mr. O'Neil stated that it is still not clear what the powers would be. The Court stated that it will recess this case, that the parties are to go outside and talk about the proposed order, if not it will set a process to get a stipulated solution and set in motion the larger issue of mediating the documents. Upon return, Ms. O'Keeffe informed the court that they have a stipulation for today, that Mr. Piggee is to be appointed as administrator and that he will not dispose of anything out of the house. The Court granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to receipt/review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kinnear. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-11-2016 Continued To Mar-21-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary Gemma O'Keeffe for and with (proposed executor) Thomas Piggee. James O'Neil for and with Treana Wilson and Clarice Hankins.

The Court stated that this will need to be continued to a date in March to have the summons served. Mr. O'Neil stated that they have made their point and produced evidence. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that no evidence has been produced and that Mr. Piggee submitted a declaration. Mr. O'Neil stated that he does not have Mr. Piggee's declaration, that he will converse with Ms. O'Keeffe and that there is not much else to be done. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that she is happy to talk with them, that the allegation is with the will, that there is no evidence that there is anything subsequent to that, that they do know that there is a retirement account but that they are unable to get information as Mr. Piggee has not been appointed. Mr. O'Neil stated that Ms. O'Keeffe has access to all of the information as she did the estate planning. The Court asked if informal mediation would be helpful. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that it will be helpful to talk and that she is glad that they are present this morning. The Court stated that it hopes she will provide any additional information that Mr. O'Neil needs. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to March 21, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the summons to be served.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-14-2015 Continued To Jan-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mary O'Keeffe for and with (petitioner) Thomas Piggee. James O'Neil for (objector) Treana Wilson.

Mr. O'Neil confirmed that he filed and sent out the will contest and summons on Friday, that he expects discovery will be necessary and requested a continuance to February 29, 2016. Ms. O'Keeffe stated that she is unclear on the basis of the will contest at this time as she has not received it. The Court stated that it will continue the matter with time for responses. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Henry. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-30-2015 Continued To Dec-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Treana Wilson and Demetriess Wilson appeared to object to the granting of the order.
The Court continued the hearing on the petition to December 14, 2015 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204, with directions that any objection to the petition be filed and served by December 9, 2015.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares.
